title: The Hungry Winter
style_prefix: epic fantasy oil painting in the style of classic 1980s fantasy game box art, thick visible brushstrokes, dramatic rim lighting, muted earthy palette, cinematic composition
negative: text, letters, words, watermark, signature, caption, cartoon, emoji, flat vector art, chibi, cute, gore, blood, deformed, extra limbs, photo, photorealistic

frames.prompt: a small medieval farming village at night in falling snow, warm lantern-lit windows, seen from across a half-frozen stream, dark vast forest looming on the far horizon, ominous quiet, first snow of winter
frames.narration: This is how the villagers tell the story. That winter, the first snow had barely fallen — and the wolves came.

frames.prompt: a broken wooden livestock pen at dawn after snowfall, shattered fence rails, deep wolf paw prints in the snow leading away toward the dark forest, villagers with lanterns gathering in the cold morning light
frames.narration: On the first night, the pen was broken and two cattle were gone. Paw prints in the snow led all the way into the dark forest.

frames.prompt: villagers gathered in grief on a snowy field ridge around a fallen figure covered with a cloth, a worn straw hat lying in the snow beside them, lantern light in blue winter dusk, sorrowful mood
frames.narration: On the third night, it was old Elm who fell. The gentlest farmer in the village — a man who had never once taken from the forest.

frames.prompt: inside a wooden cottage at night, a mother holding a small child close by the shuttered window, a father barring the heavy door, firelight flickering on worried faces, snow drifting past the window crack
frames.narration: From that night on, no one dared go out after dark. Every door was barred, and every fire was kept burning until dawn.

frames.prompt: an old village elder in a hooded brown cloak standing on wooden steps handing hunting bows to a crowd of villagers, hunters with faces hidden in hood shadows, a sea of torches in the snowy night, grim determination
frames.narration: The elder handed out the hunting bows, one by one. If they cross the stream, he said, they will learn whose home this is.

frames.prompt: hunters with bows on a wooden palisade wall at night, arrows nocked toward the dark forest, torchlight and falling snow, and far away in the black treeline a pair of glowing amber eyes watching
frames.narration: The night watchman swore the wolf king saw the bow and stepped back — then slipped into the shadows. Vermin, they called him.

frames.prompt: a snowy valley seen from high above at night, a winding half-frozen stream dividing the land, small warm village lights on one side far below, vast dark wild forest filling the other side, the composition facing the forest
frames.narration: That is the village's version. Now let us turn this winter back — and tell it once more, from the other side of the stream.

frames.prompt: gaunt grey wolves sniffing at an empty rabbit burrow under snow-covered tree roots in a bare winter forest, thin cold light, ribs showing under fur, and half-buried in snow in the far background a rusted iron trap
frames.narration: Before winter ever came, the forest was already starving. The burrows emptied, one by one. Wolves do not ask why. They simply hunger.

frames.prompt: a wolf den beneath dark rocks in winter forest, a mother wolf curled around three small wolf pups, above on the ridge a large grey wolf with amber eyes and a jagged old claw scar on its left shoulder standing watch, gazing at distant village lights across the valley, night
frames.narration: In the den there were three pups. That night, the wolf king stood on the ridge until dawn. Across the stream, the cattle had grown too fat to walk.

frames.prompt: a pack of gaunt grey wolves crossing a half-frozen stream at night, led by a large grey wolf with amber eyes and a jagged old claw scar on its left shoulder, the pack moving inside the long moon-cast shadows of trees, falling snow, quiet tension
frames.narration: He too once had brothers. They died under the bows of men. So he walks in the shadows — and he taught his whole pack to walk in them.

frames.prompt: inside a dim wolf den in winter, a grey wolf lowering food to three hungry wolf pups pressing close, breath visible in the cold, snow at the den mouth, intimate warm-in-cold mood
frames.narration: What the village saw was slaughter. What the pups in the den saw was the one mouthful of meat that would carry them through the winter.

frames.prompt: a grey wolf lying still in falling snow with a single arrow in its flank, the rest of the wolf pack standing at the dark treeline looking back in silence, cold blue night, grief
frames.narration: On the fifth night, Grey Claw did not come home. When a wolf is lost, it is lost for good.

frames.prompt: two mirrored scenes in one painting divided by a frozen stream: on the village side mourners around a fresh grave with a straw hat resting on a plain unmarked wooden marker, on the forest side the wolf pack standing in silent vigil around a fallen wolf lying in the snow, its grey fur slowly being covered by the falling snow, the same snow falling over both, elegiac symmetry
frames.narration: The same snow buried old Elm — and buried Grey Claw. Both sides were protecting something. Both sides were bleeding.

frames.prompt: a large grey wolf with amber eyes and a jagged old claw scar on its shoulder facing an old hooded village elder holding a lantern across a frozen stream, night and moonlight on the wolf side, warm dawn light on the village side, snow falling between them, neither backing down, epic symmetrical confrontation
frames.narration: The snow is still falling. The wolf king and the elder face each other across the stream, and neither will yield. This land cannot hold two ways of living. And if it were you, standing in their place?
